name: Query Engine
description: Nexora Engine's smart query system with automatic caching, deduplication, and TTL-based expiration.

intent.what: Provides a high-level cached query layer on top of Supabase's native query capabilities. Supports automatic request deduplication, TTL-based caching, and cache invalidation on mutations.
intent.why: Reduces redundant database calls, improves response times, and provides a clean API for building complex queries without manual cache management.

steps: Call queryEngine.query() with table name and options
Results are automatically cached based on query parameters
Use queryEngine.invalidateTable() to manually clear cache
code_examples: const users = await queryEngine.query<User>({ table: 'users', filters: [{ column: 'status', operator: 'eq', value: 'active' }], ttl: 60000 })
const result = await queryEngine.queryPaginated<User>({ table: 'users', page: 1, pageSize: 20 })

internal_flow: Check cache for existing result using derived cache key
If cached and not expired, return cached data
If concurrent query exists, deduplicate by returning existing promise
Store result in cache with TTL
For mutations: invalidate related cache entries automatically
logic.cache_key_derivation: table + operation + filters + sort + pagination + columns
logic.invalidation: Mutations call invalidateTable() which clears all cache keys matching the table pattern

rules: bypassCache true skips cache read but still writes on successful response
Pagination uses limit and offset internally
anti_patterns: Don't use for real-time data that changes rapidly
Don't set very long TTLs for frequently changing data
Don't call invalidateTable() in rapid succession

when_to_use: Use for most read operations that can benefit from caching
Use for list/detail views in UI applications
Use for API endpoints serving relatively static data
when_not_to_use: Don't use for real-time data (use subscriptions)
Don't use for mutations directly (use mutation methods)
Don't use when absolute freshness needed (use bypassCache true)
reasoning_strategy: Determine if data can be cached (relatively static?)
Choose appropriate TTL based on data volatility
Use query() for lists, querySingle() for single records
For mutations, use built-in methods to auto-invalidate
For manual cache control, use invalidateTable() or invalidateAll()
